# ADR-010 Compliance — Observability & Reliability

**Governance phase:** 3.5 · **Authority document:** `docs/ADR-010-*.md` · **Date:** 2026-07-19
**Verifier:** `architecture/compliance/verify-architecture.mjs` · **Do not edit the ADR itself.**

## Purpose
Make the platform observable: health, metrics, logs, tracing, SLOs, alerting.

## Scope
Enterprise observability & reliability architecture. No vendor prescription.

## Current implementation status
**Proposed; Partial telemetry — 40% compliant.**

## Implemented components
- /health with db/memory/event-loop checks (503 on degrade)
- In-process metrics middleware + /admin/metrics
- Structured logging with rotation + security events
- Monitoring compose (Prometheus/Grafana) present

## Remaining work
- Scrapeable prom-client exposition (M-5)
- Distributed tracing / request-id propagation across contexts
- SLOs + alerting rules

## Evidence
- src/routes/health.js live 200/503
- docker-compose.monitoring.yml (217L)
- logger security events

## Related source code
- `src/routes/health.js`
- `src/middleware/metrics.js`
- `src/utils/logger.js`
- `docker-compose.monitoring.yml`

## Related tests
- (none yet — covered by review/live proof)

## Compliance percentage
**40%** — honest estimate: weight of ADR requirements realized under the ADR-005
layered architecture with evidence, vs. total requirements named in the ADR.

## Risks
- Metrics not scrapeable by Prometheus (M-5) — monitoring stack has no app target

## Required actions
- Add prom-client /metrics exposition on internal network
